scope_note: QA Witt Tower Fibonacci-Pisano Synthesis cert [441]. Applies the complete Witt tower orbit chain [437]-[440] to characterise the Pisano period pi(p^k) — the period of the Fibonacci sequence mod p^k — for all primes p and all k>=1. The Fibonacci companion matrix M=[[1,1],[1,0]] has char poly x^2-x-1 (det=-1, t=1); its matrix order on (Z/p^k Z)^2 equals the Pisano period pi(p^k). THREE REGIMES: (1) p=5 RAMIFIED (the unique Fibonacci-ramified prime, since disc=t^2+4=5 and v_5(5)=1=r): governed by [440] with det=-1, r=1; pi(5^k)=4*5^k (max orbit = 4*5^L for L=k, counting 5^(k-1) birth orbits). (2) p=2 EXCEPTIONAL: pi(2^k)=3*2^(k-1) for k>=1 (pi(2)=3); p=2 is not covered by [440] (requires p odd, p≡1 mod 4); handled by the separate p=2 Jordan-block law noted in [435]. (3) p!=2,5 UNRAMIFIED: pi(p^k)=pi(p)*p^(k-1) (Wall (1960) lift law); the companion matrix is diagonalizable mod p (split case, Legendre(5,p)=+1, pi(p)|p-1) or inert (Legendre(5,p)=-1, pi(p)|2(p+1)); no Witt tower ramification correction needed. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) p=5, k=1..5: max orbit of Fibonacci companion = pi(5^k) = 4*5^k; brute-force Pisano confirms. (C2) p=5, k=1..4: orbit count distribution matches [440] formula (r=1) exactly. (C3) p=2, k=1..8: pi(2^k) = 3*2^(k-1) (known formula, verified brute-force). (C4) p in {3,7,11,13,17,19}, k=2..4: pi(p^k)=pi(p)*p^(k-1) (Wall lift law). (C5) p in {2,3,5,7,11,13}, k=1..3: max orbit of Fibonacci companion = pi(p^k) across all regimes. (C6) Legendre(5,p) classification: pi(p)|p-1 for split primes, pi(p)|2(p+1) for inert primes; 12 primes verified. PRIMARY SOURCES: Wall, D.D. (1960) American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (pi(p^k)=pi(p)*p^(k-1) for unramified p); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.5,7 (Legendre symbol, quadratic residues); Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (p-adic ramification). Derived 2026-06-17.
